scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE CITY CIVIL COURT AT Bengaluru

O.S. No. Commercial Suit No. __________ / 20____
RAM KUMAR, Aged 45 years, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, Plaintiff

-VERSUS-

Suresh Patel, Aged 38 years, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, Defendant

JURISDICTION

City Civil Court Bengaluru, Karnataka, India

FACTS OF THE CASE

1. The Plaintiff advanced Rs. 15,00,000 to the Defendant by NEFT on 15.03.2024 as a hand loan. There was no written agreement.
2. The loan is evidenced by the NEFT record and WhatsApp acknowledgments annexed as Annexure B.
3. The Defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages.
4. Repeated oral demands were made and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 demanding repayment was served.
5. The Defendant defaulted on 20.06.2024; the Plaintiff is entitled to recover the principal plus lawful interest.
6. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 demanding repayment of Rs. 15,00,000 along with interest at 12 percent per annum from 20.06.2024 till payment; Annexure C attached.
7. The Defendant failed to repay the principal or any interest despite service of the legal notice.
8. The Plaintiff seeks to recover the principal sum of Rs. 15,00,000 with interest at 12 percent per annum from 20.06.2024 till realization and costs.

LEGAL BASIS

9. The loan evidenced by NEFT transfer and WhatsApp acknowledgments creates a contract between the Plaintiff and Defendant. {{PROVISION_1}}

CAUSE OF ACTION

10. The cause of action first arose on 20.06.2024 when the Defendant defaulted in returning the loan amount and interest as agreed. 11. The cause of action further arose on 01.07.2024 when the Plaintiff caused a legal notice demanding repayment. 12. The cause of action is continuing as the Defendant retains the Plaintiff’s money without justification.

LIMITATION

Article 22 of the Limitation Act, 1963 — three years. The suit is within three years from the date of accrual (20.06.2024) and the notice dated 01.07.2024; accordingly, the suit is within time.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

The suit is valued at Rs. 15,00,000. The Court Fee payable shall be calculated as ad valorem on the value of Rs. 15,00,000 as applicable under the Karnataka Court Fees Act, with the final amount to be determined before filing.

INTEREST

The Plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum on the principal amount of Rs. 15,00,000 from 20.06.2024 until realization; pendente lite interest at such rate as this Court may deem just under Section 34 CPC; future interest at 12% per annum from the date of decree till realization.

PRAYER

The Plaintiff prays that this Hon’ble Court may be pleased to:
(a) Pass a decree for recovery of Rs. 15,00,000 with interest at 12% p.a. from 20.06.2024 till realization and costs;
(b) Award pre-suit interest at 12% p.a. from 20.06.2024 to the date of filing;
(c) Award pendente lite interest under Section 34 of the CPC at such rate as this Court may deem just and reasonable during the pendency of the suit;
(d) Award future interest at 12% p.a. from the date of decree till realization;
(e) Award costs of the suit including legal notice costs;
(f) Grant such other and further relief as this Hon’ble Court may deem fit in the facts and circumstances of the case;
(g) Direct the parties to mediation under Section 12A of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015 prior to final admission, in accordance with the mandatory pre-institution mediation provisions.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

[Annexure A: NEFT transfer record with UTR AXIB20240315123456; Annexure B: WhatsApp messages; Annexure C: Legal notice dated 01.07.2024; Annexure D: Postal receipt; Annexure E: Response (or lack thereof)]

VERIFICATION

I, Ram Kumar, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify and state on oath that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 12 are true and correct to my personal knowledge based on records and documents available with me; that the facts relating to legal submissions are based on legal advice received and believed to be correct; that no part of the cause of action has arisen in any other court's jurisdiction; and that nothing material has been concealed therefrom. Verified at Bengaluru on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

Ram Kumar
Plaintiff

Through:
{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
Advocate
Enrollment No. {{ADVOCATE_ENROLLMENT}}
{{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}
